item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd really like to hear more about the SCP Corporation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really interesting exploration of alternate foundations

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn’t mind seeing more either, but I would also like to see the author expand on the existing Parallel Universes.

Edit: Sorry for the Necropost…
Thank you so much to everyone who has read this over the years!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The images that were used in this article have been replaced with the following images, which are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

+ Show attribution
- Hide attribution

Source: Geograph
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: Kirstan's Hole
Author: Anne Burgess
Release year: 2006

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Guatemala City 2010 sinkhole 1.jpg
Author: Simon Burchell
Release year: 2010

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Azazel IMG 1771.JPG
Author: deror_avi
Release year: 2010

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: Public Domain
Title: Mosul Dam sinkhole.jpg
Author: United States Army Corps of Engineers
Release year: 2007

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Title: 181 Мирный. Трубка «Мир».jpg
Author: A. L. (loading)
Release year: 2016

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely love this but one little thing bothers me. It doesn't seem like the 4th alternate reality mentioned would have sent that document.

By the time it was last updated they didn't trust the "gifts" so they probably wouldn't want to send that document.

item.title: More stuff that could have fallen through
item.content:encoded:
This is some more stuff that might have fallen through. I'll let you ponder it.

  • Approximately eight hundred liters of water and about eleven hundred instances of a yellow and purple shrimp species
  • Fourteen banana peels and a receipt written in a similar language to ancient latin for fourteen bananas and some salted meat
  • A large blue boulder
  • A tracking device attached to a rope, torn after about twenty meters of length
  • Fifty kilograms of dirt and a partially crushed organism resembling a puffin, but seemingly adapted to a life of burrowing underground, with only vestigial wings and with a tail 20 centimeters long.
  • Eight cubes, each one cubic centimeter, of pure uranium
  • A partially-crushed orange metal plate depicting four yellow tentacled organisms, several mathematical formulas written in base-9, a solar system of six planets, and several paragraphs of an unknown language with at least fifty-seven different characters
  • A heavily damaged metal detector covered in gold plates with several large dents on its handle
  • A rusted hollow iron sphere made entirely of iron, exactly one meter in diameter
  • A green yoyo with the message "Thanks for having a fun time at ScipWorld!" printed on the front and "PROPORTY OF MARL" [sic] written in black sharpie on the back side
  • A wooden box with four syringes filled with non-anomalous water. The syringes each possess a label reading "Y-class Mnestics"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the fact that there is a universe where the foundation decided to make some SCPs "safe for public" instead of just hiding everything away. Kinda makes you think..

item.title: Re: Y'know what this SCP would be great for?
item.content:encoded: I'd actually like a story where they somehow find a way to get to the other side of the hole and meet up with them, maybe the RPC can even make a 1437 on their wiki

item.title: Y'know what this SCP would be great for?
item.content:encoded: An RPC Authority crossover!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how it's possible some people probably worship this hole in ground. +1 vote.
We made it, baby!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip is brilliantly constructed and well-executed. It deserves a higher rating than it has.

+1

item.title: Half-decent idea.
item.content:encoded:
These Bizarro foundations have good take potential, and I think have some good ideas.

A field trip in the First Universe ends up bringing kids to see some terrifying shit.

SCP Inc. tries to sell an odd-looking statue.

The High Priest attempts to vanquish the Orange Blob demon.

… I don't know what to do with the last one.

Feel free to take these ideas! I think they'd work best in a short story format in one large tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like to ask the creator if you would let me create another one of alternate universe documents. I really like that idea and would like to expand on it. My idea of a note would be to write it all in another language. Ive taken a year of German, so I could write it for you with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How is this safe when at least one bomb has been sent through it? Surely, any portal to sentient entities must be Euclid.

Also, I what to see more SCPs from the perspective of the alternate foundations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so…
four alternate versions of the SCP foundation

One has almost no concern for secrecy of safety and gives tours for their SCP's.
Disney Foundation

Another is a greedy corporate that economically exploits SCP's.
SCP Inc,.

Yet another is a magic cult whose job is too banish the demonic forces (SCP's)
Sacred Cult of Powers

A creepy and evil people who worship demon man-eating demon gods (presumably the enemies of the Sacred Cult of Powers)
Serve Capture and Procreate?

More versions of the SCP foundation please

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: they successfully managed to create an scp out of an actual hole in the ground. And they did it accross the multiverse. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you dig into it from the side, there's just solid rock, right? What if you dig up in that position? Where do you come out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If only I could upvote once for each addenda… This was some wonderfully brilliant world-building. It's hard to decide which bit was my favorite, but the note about the soccer ball mad me laugh my ass off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just curious, because The Church of the Foundation was a cannon in which the Foundation was a religeous group instead of a science research facility, dealing with anomalies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They were just parallel universes. I don't think they were canons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So is the documents that were brought into the main Foundation-verse, did they come from different cannons of the SCP Foundation? Because, one of them I recognize as the The Church of the Foundation.

What were the other three?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it so much, but I'll have to correct the spanish. (We don't capitalize each word in a title, just the first one, or proper names. In this case the article wouldn't be capitalized)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, this thing just keeps getting better and better…
From this Foundation
to less-secret Foundation
to Foundation MC&D edition
to Foundation priests edition
to Foundation alien takeover
Also who the heck is sending all those gold disks
+1 for sure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Need moar univereses. NAO!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• A sheet of paper reading "THANK YOU PLEASE SEND MORE GIFTS."
  • The lower half of a human body. (Delivered to supervising Servant of Our Masters Above. It is currently unknown how the gift-sender was aware of the diet of Our Masters Above. A document was sent into SCP-1437 thanking the gift-giver for the object and requesting further tributes to Our Masters Above.)

the little details make this even better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, the documents at the end were a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This IS much better than 685

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice. That was a very entertaining read, also I loved what you did with the articles. I did notice that it was called the same thing in every dimension, but it didn't bother me too much, afterall, you don't won't people thinking you're talking about something else.

Really great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the danger of the thing is reliant on someone being near (and often, oblivious) enough to trigger some sort of danger, then it's Safe class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because the object class isn't based on how dangerous it is, but on how easy it is to contain. In this case, since it's not going anywhere, a simple fence and a few guards would suffice. Hence the safe designation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the object class safe when it seems as though anyone who falls through it dies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the best. A true work of [DATA EXPUNGED] +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the please send more gifts thing reminded me of the outer limits episode with the well and the turkey, awesome scp all around. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait is 682 one of the masters above?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would pay top dollar for more Emerged Documentation of this caliber. It's fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This thing seems really useful for dumping Keter class that require termination, or is that too impolite to those alternate foundation? I'd like it if 682 got dumped into it, and after a while, we get another version of that thing back.
Edit: whoops, MobiusStripe got this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kept thinking they shoulda sent some rain. Not that that would be in keeping with things… but still. Kinda sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heathen Level: Unforgivable

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that it is apparently impossible to travel through SCP-1437 and survive has prevented the Corporation from utilizing SCP-1437 for commercial purposes.

Foundation/Corporation tried to do what!?!?!?

Heathen Level: Unforgivable.

Doesnt get much better than this.

■A human eye which moved when touched. (Believed to be a lesser demon. Was burnt immediately.)

Um… most things move when touched.

On ██/██/██, a bottle of human-intended wine emerged from SCP-1437. Recognizing an assassination attempt upon members of Our Masters Above, the supervising Servant ordered that a Don-Burten explosive device be delivered down SCP-1437. Its orders were followed and the threat appears to have been resolved.

This is exactly what I think when neighbours come over and try to give me wine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ■A cube composed of several smaller cubes, all of which are different colours. (Purpose unknown.) this is excellent

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: About 682, while it isn't mentioned by name, it seems to be known by the people of other dimensions (the statue found by SCP corporation).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this article, but going to add in my thoughts that this should be Euclid, if not Keter - or at least have stronger containment.

What of another universe sent one of the following things through:

*Their version of 682
*A nuclear bomb
*A nanomachine plague.
*Some sort of horrific infohazard
*Anything capable of causing an NK event (Even if it wasn't deliberate; if another universe was covered in, for example, SCP-505, wouldn't it eventually start pouring through and possibly entering our universe?)
*Self replicating killer robots

And yet all we have to cover this is 3 security officers and an electric fence?

Still upvoting, I love this article so hard, but thought I should mention that. And I want more universes! So many more universes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The truth is out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will! Thanks a lot for the information. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In case the author cares, the Spanish for "The People's Republic of Aguas Nuevas" is "La República Popular De Aguas Nuevas". You can put that in the article if you'd like (e.g.: According to said map, the government of North America is "La República Popular De Aguas Nuevas", or "The Peoples Republic of New Waters".)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Don-Burten explosive device"

I've scoured the internet, trying to find a proposal or something this could've been inspired by, and I've found absolutely shit. I MUST KNOW!
Why hadn't I upvoted this yet? Anyway, +250 net votes are something to celebrate. Congrats, author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Carnally?

Edit: considering your comment on 113, I'd say yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP on so many different levels +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure what the original article looked like, but it's certainly no accident that six documents all read 'where logic would dictate'. Alone, it is idiosyncratic and erroneous. With the following articles, it is thought-provoking and a clever tidbit. Makes you wonder if there's a multiversal character where nature supercedes nurture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was one of my early favorites. Thank you Cymon for reminding me that it exists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice use of a bunch of sink-hole pictures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like to think that they have different words in the SCP acronym. If you use a different one, there's no chance to explain what it stands for in the traditional SCP format.

Documentation 3, for example, could be in the Sanctify, Cleanse, Pray Foundation. Or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it would be neat if the Mayan universe of naked people (or whatever it is, the third universe) Didn't have a format, because of the way they act.

Also, why are all their SCP foundations called SCP? I'm pretty sure one or two of the universe don't even use the words "Secure, Contain, Protect" Unless their version of SCP stands for something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this article. The alternate universes' versions of the SCP foundation - including the one ruled by some sort of religious cult, and the one where wine is apparently an assassination attempt - are very clever. I also liked how items from one universe were found and analyzed in other universes entirely, such as the "PLEASE SEND MORE GIFTS" paper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Implying Class D's are people.
:rolls eyes:
